generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize resolving unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations when AGREE reports type errors.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (const, eq) use valid scalar types: int, real, or bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for assumptions and guarantees.
Define equations with `eq name : type = expression;`
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing new AADL architecture to fix AGREE contract errors.
Do not attempt to use implementation-level details within component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
